title: Jennifer Garner and Judy Greer to Appear in Netflix’s ’13 Going on 30′ Reboot
description: Jennifer Garner and Judy Greer were spotted on set of Netflix’s “13 Going on 30,” filming a scene with Emily Bader, who stars in the new reimagining
body:
Jennifer Garner and Judy Greer were spotted on set of Netflix’s “13 Going on 30,” filming a scene with Emily Bader, who stars in the new reimagining with Logan Lerman. 

	Garner — who played Jenna Rink, the awkward teenager magically transported into her 30-year-old life as a high-powered, but messy, magazine editor — was already on board the project as an executive producer. Last week, she told Variety’s Marc Malkin that she would be visiting the set. 
	

	“I’m going to go [and] see if I can bump around and see if they’ll put me in somewhere,” Garner joked. “Maybe I could be in the background of whatever the ‘Thriller’ scene is.”



	But it appears Garner was just playing coy. The set photos, published on Tuesday by Just Jared, show Garner, Bader and Greer — who played Jenna’s frenemy, Lucy “Tom-Tom” Wyman — in costume and filming a scene at a building in Santa Monica, Calif. Netflix has not confirmed that they’re reprising their roles, and the project’s logline is being kept under wraps, so it’s unclear how much Garner and Greer factor into the plot.

	The new “13 Going on 30” is directed by Brett Haley, who most recently helmed Netflix’s adaptation of Emily Henry’s “People We Met On Vacation,” starring Bader, which was a massive hit. Taylor Zakhar Perez, Jessica Alba, Adeline Rudolph, Taylor Ortega, Tim Meadows and Dan Bucatinsky will also star in the movie. 2004’s “13 Going on 30,” directed by Gary Winick and also starring Mark Ruffalo, grossed $96 million in theaters and became a cultural touchstone for Millennial audiences.
	

	In April, Garner told Variety why she championed the reboot 22 years after the original comedy hit theaters.

	“It’s the first of many, many times that someone has brought [a reboot] up to me, so it started to feel like — I don’t want it to happen without me participating,” she said. “Jenna is so close to me, but there’s a musical [adaptation] happening that’s going to be in Toronto. There was a TV show that I was a part of working toward that never really got off the ground. … And, in this case, of course, I said I was interested.”

	The script and Bader, whom she described as “magical,” clinched it for her. “I want to watch her do it,” Garner continued. “I mean, why should it just be me? Why can’t magic strike twice? Why can’t she have an incredible experience with it in a way that audiences can relate all over again?”

	Greer concurred, adding: “It’s been ours for so long. Let’s let someone else have a crack at it.”

	At the time, there weren’t plans for Garner and Greer, who were promoting their latest collaboration in Apple TV’s “The Last Thing He Told Me,” to reprise their roles. But if the opportunity presented itself, both actresses were game. “I would do literally anything that Jennifer Garner asked me to do,” Greer teased. “And since she’s the executive producer, she would be my boss again, and I would do whatever she wanted me to do.”

	Netflix had no comment regarding the casting.

schema.headline: Jennifer Garner and Judy Greer to Appear in Netflix’s ’13 Going on 30′ Reboot

title: Games for Change 2026 Award Winners Revealed
description:
The nonprofit organization Games for Change announced the winners of the 2026 Games for Change Awards at the G4C Awards Ceremony.

	The ceremony was
body:
The nonprofit organization Games for Change announced the winners of the 2026 Games for Change Awards at the G4C Awards Ceremony.

	The ceremony was held during the annual Games for Change Festival in New York, hosted by actors Anjali Bhimani (“Overwatch,” “Ms. Marvel”) and Osric Chau (“Supernatural,” “Dirk Gently’s Holistic Detective Agency”).

	“It has been a remarkable year for games made with purpose, and these winners show the breadth of it,” said Games for Change President Susanna Pollack. “They come from studios and institutions across four continents, each one turning to a game or interactive experience to say something that mattered to them. That is what we’re celebrating– how global this work has become.”
	

	The 2026 award winners are:


	Game/Experience of the Year

	South of Midnight (Compulsion Games)

	Best Narrative, Best Gameplay, Indie Breakout Award

	Consume Me (Hexecutable)

	Best in Impact

	and Roger (TearyHand Studio)

	Best in Learning

	The Darkest Files (Paintbucket Games)

	Best in Community Empowerment

	Relooted (Nyamakop)

	Best in Environmental Impact

	Faceminer (Wristwork)

	Best in Health & Wellness

	ARWell PRO (Augment Therapy)

	Best in XR & Emerging Media

	A Long Goodbye (Timescapes & Polygoat)

	Best Platform-Based Project

	Planet Planners on Roblox (Playerthree & BBC Bitesize)

	Best Board or Tabletop Game for Impact

	Ciclo do Poder / Cycle of Power (Fiocruz)

	The G4C Awards also recognized special award honorees, selected for their broader contributions to games and social impact.
	

	The 2026 special award winners are:

	Vanguard Award

	Jenn Panattoni

	Industry Leadership Award

	thatgamecompany

	Giving Award

	SpecialEffect

	Accessibility Award

	EA Sports FC 26 (EA Sports)

schema.headline: Games for Change 2026 Award Winners Revealed

title: Mike Preston, ‘Mad Max 2: The Road Warrior’ and ‘Hot Pursuit’ Actor, Dies at 93
description: Mike Preston, known for playing settler leader Pappagallo in “Mad Max 2: The Road Warrior” and Detective Bob Delaney in the Australian series “Homic
body:
Mike Preston, known for playing settler leader Pappagallo in “Mad Max 2: The Road Warrior” and Detective Bob Delaney in the Australian series “Homicide,” died June 8 in Florida. He was 93.

	Preston’s death was confirmed to Variety by his wife, Josie Preston.

	Preston, who was born Jack Davis, was an early U.K. chart-topping singer in the late ’50s with three Top 40 hits. He then relocated to Australia and began working as a nightclub singer and performer.
	

	He found success hosting “In Melbourne Tonight,” before going on to appear in 42 episodes of the Australian series “Homicide.” He had a recurring role in the soap opera “Bellbird” and also appeared in “Dixon of Dock Green,” “Tandarra,” “Chopper Squad” and “Cop Shop.”


	His first feature film role was in 1969’s “Stoney,” which also featured Barbara Bouchet and Richard Jaeckel. He worked with George Miller on the beloved “Mad Max” sequel “The Road Warrior,” led the sci-fi flick “Metalstorm: The Destruction of Jared-Syn” as the titular character, and appeared in eight episodes of “Hot Pursuit” as Alec Shaw, alongside actors like Kerrie Keane, Eric Pierpoint and Dina Merrill.

	Throughout the ’80s and ’90s, Preston appeared in guest spots on over a dozen shows, including “Ellen,” “Scarecrow and Mrs. King,” “Shadow Chasers,” “Outlaws,” “Superbly,” “Alien Nation,” “Dark Justice,” “Highlander,” “Steel” and “Law & Order.” His last credit was a voice role in the video game “The Getaway,” released in 2002.

	Preston is survived by his wife, Josie Preston, and two daughters.

schema.headline: Mike Preston, ‘Mad Max 2: The Road Warrior’ and ‘Hot Pursuit’ Actor, Dies at 93

title: Sony Music Files Another Lawsuit Against Udio, Alleges AI Music Generator Copied 30,000 Songs to Train Models
description: Sony Music Entertainment has once again sued the AI music generator Udio, alleging the startup illegally copied more than 30,000 songs from the like
body:
Sony Music Entertainment has once again sued the AI music generator Udio, alleging the startup illegally copied more than 30,000 songs from the likes of Beyoncé, Harry Styles and Elvis Presley to train its models.

	Sony first brought lawsuits against Udio and its competitor Suno in June 2024 alongside labels Universal Music Group and Warner Music Group. In the new lawsuit, filed Monday in the U.S. District Court of the Southern District of New York, Sony claimed that discovery for the case revealed 30,117 tracks were found among Udio’s training datasets. U.S. District Court Judge Alvin K. Hellerstein last month denied Sony’s bid to add those tracks to its original suit, prompting the label to file a new complaint to protect its copyright.
	

	“Foundational principles of copyright law dictate that copying protected sound recordings for the purpose of developing a commercial AI product like Udio’s requires permission from rightsholders,” Sony alleged in its complaint. “Otherwise, such AI offerings will erode the value of the artistic works that comprise the essential raw materials that allow them to function in the first place. If left unmoored from existing and longstanding legal constraints, such products could supplant, rather than support, genuine human creativity.”


	Representatives for Sony and Udio did not respond to immediate requests for comment. Udio claimed in an April response to Sony’s initial suit that, while admitting it trained its models on copyrighted songs uploaded to YouTube, such training amounted to fair use due to its platform using “a back-end technological process, invisible to the public, in the service of creating an ultimately non-infringing new product.”
	

	The case comes as companies across the media and entertainment industries, from Hollywood studios to record labels to news organizations, try to navigate how to engage with AI while protecting their copyrighted work. Since the original lawsuit, both Universal Music Group and Warner Music Group have settled their respective cases against Udio and entered into licensing agreements with the AI startup, which is preparing a new industry-backed model for release later this year. (Sony and Universal are still fighting Suno in court.)

	But such agreements show that Udio could have licensed Sony’s tracks instead of allegedly ripping them from YouTube, the label claimed.

	“At its core, this case is about ensuring that copyright continues to incentivize human invention and imagination, as it has for centuries,” Sony claimed in its lawsuit. “Achieving this end does not require stunting technological innovation, but it does require that Udio adhere to copyright law and respect the creators whose works allow it to function in the first place.”

	The company is seeking at least $150,000 per each work infringed and for the court to block Udio from using its work to train its models.

schema.headline: Sony Music Files Another Lawsuit Against Udio, Alleges AI Music Generator Copied 30,000 Songs to Train Models

title: Jennifer Garner Rails Against Paparazzi Who ‘Knocked Over’ Kids and More Just to Get Photos of Her and Ben Affleck: ‘So Hungry to Get at Me’
description:
Jennifer Garner recently told the “Shut Up Evan” podcast that paparazzi would go to dangerous lengths to get photos of her and her children.

	“If I
body:
Jennifer Garner recently told the “Shut Up Evan” podcast that paparazzi would go to dangerous lengths to get photos of her and her children.

	“If I went through a yellow light, 15 cars would go through the red behind me,” Garner said. “They would drive up onto people’s lawns, even on a hillside. We were trying to have our little one play soccer, and the association asked us to stop because there were 20 cars coming. So it wasn’t just about us. It was just an industry that had gotten out of control.”
	

	She added, “If you show up and you’re trying to get into the pediatrician and you have a sick kid on your shoulder and you’re scared and you can’t get into the door because you’re blocked by photographers, there’s something wrong with that, and the kids are paying the price.”


	Garner also recalled a time when she saw kids “getting knocked over coming out of preschool by photographers who were so hungry to get at me or Ben [Affleck].” She went on to explain that, despite living in a community with many celebrities, the paparazzi would target her because she “had the kids.”

	When asked by host Evan Katz if things ever got better, Garner said she still has two photographers who have been assigned to follow her for “20 plus years.” She says in that time, they’ve developed an “odd respect for each other.

	“Like, there’s kind of a Stockholm syndrome thing,” Garner said. “And at the same time, I’ve called the cops on him a million times and been like, ‘Can you please just give me a walk around the block with my kids and my dog?’”

schema.headline: Jennifer Garner Rails Against Paparazzi Who ‘Knocked Over’ Kids and More Just to Get Photos of Her and Ben Affleck: ‘So Hungry to Get at Me’

title: Indie LGBTQ Dramedy ‘Actual Nobodies’ Acquired by Scatena & Rosner Films, Sets Early 2027 Release (EXCLUSIVE)
description: Scatena & Rosner Films has acquired distribution rights for North America, Australia and New Zealand for the LGBTQ indie dramedy “Actual Nobodies,”
body:
Scatena & Rosner Films has acquired distribution rights for North America, Australia and New Zealand for the LGBTQ indie dramedy “Actual Nobodies,” written and directed by Alex Wyse. S&R picked the film up at this year’s Cannes Market.

	Germany’s Media Luna New Films has secured rights for all remaining territories. A U.S. release is planned for early 2027.

	Marking Wyse’s solo feature directorial debut following the GLAAD Media Award-nominated and ARTIOS-winning “Summoning Sylvia,” “Actual Nobodies” stars legendary actress Didi Conn (“Grease,” “Best Medicine”) and Austin P. McKenzie (“When We Rise,” “Speech & Debate”).
	

	“Actual Nobodies” tells the story of two lonely outsiders who find unexpected companionship when Joyce (Conn), a whimsical woman in her seventies, rents out her spare room to Anthony (McKenzie), a young gay man in search of solitude.


	The ensemble also includes Greta Quispe (“American Fiction,” “New Amsterdam”), Paul Lieber (“Alias,” “Barney Miller”), Will Chase (“Stranger Things,” “Dopesick”), Jason Veasey (“Only Murders in the Building,” “Best Medicine”), Olli Haaskivi (“Widow’s Bay,” “Oppenheimer”) and David T. Patterson (“The Gilded Age,” “Blue Bloods”).

	“’Actual Nobodies’ is a story about the people who feel invisible to the world and the surprising ways they ultimately connect with one another,” Wyse said in a statement Tuesday. “Making this film with this extraordinary cast and crew was deeply personal, and finding partners in Scatena & Rosner, who truly believe in filmmaker-driven, emotionally resonant storytelling, makes this next chapter especially exciting.”
	

	Gato Scatena and Jordan Rosner of Scatena & Rosner Films added: “Alex has a unique voice that balances humor and heart with incredible precision. Actual Nobodies is exactly the kind of elevated, theatrical-forward independent cinema we are passionate about championing, and we’re thrilled to have launched the film’s global journey in Cannes. Independent films don’t break through simply because they’re well made—they break through because they make audiences feel something. Actual Nobodies does exactly that.”

	“Films with genuine emotional authenticity have tremendous international appeal, and Actual Nobodies delivers exactly that. Alex Wyse has created a heartfelt, uplifting film with universal themes that we believe will resonate with international buyers and audiences alike,” Media Luna New Films’ Ida Martin said.

	Producer Cody Lassen of Alder Street Productions said, “From the moment we began sharing the film, we felt it connect with audiences in a profound way. Scatena & Rosner and Media Luna immediately understood both its emotional core and its broader marketplace potential. We’re excited to partner with them as we introduce Actual Nobodies to buyers and audiences around the world.”

	Producing alongside Tony Award-winning and Grammy-nominated Lassen’s Alder Street Productions (“Summoning Sylvia,” “Indecent”) is Trey Tetreault (“Holding Back the Tide”), and Wyse, with co-producers Neil Gooding Productions, Second Set Productions, and associate producers Rayshaun Sandlin and Pam Moriarty.

	The film features cinematography by Al Bouchillon, editing by Sara Corrigan, music by Max Mueller, costumes by Nadia Durham, and production design by Rachel Aileen Abraham. The film was shot on location in New Jersey.

	Lassen and Wyse previously collaborated on the horror-comedy feature “Summoning Sylvia,” which received a theatrical release, was acquired by Starz, and currently holds a 100% critics’ score on Rotten Tomatoes.

schema.headline: Indie LGBTQ Dramedy ‘Actual Nobodies’ Acquired by Scatena & Rosner Films, Sets Early 2027 Release (EXCLUSIVE)

title: Sony Pictures Mulls Deal for Hollywood’s Cinerama Dome, With Alamo Drafthouse Possibly Operating
description:
Sony Pictures is in early talks to lease and operate the legendary Cinerama Dome theater in Hollywood, Variety has confirmed.

	Sony has been active
body:
Sony Pictures is in early talks to lease and operate the legendary Cinerama Dome theater in Hollywood, Variety has confirmed.

	Sony has been actively moving into the exhibition business in the past few years, acquiring the Alamo Drafthouse chain in 2024 for its Sony Pictures Experiences arm and investing in Cosm shared-reality experiences earlier this year.

	Sony would operate the theaters under a lease arrangement and would not be able to use the name ArcLight, due to Chapter 7 proceedings, according to Deadline, which first reported the talks. In addition, Alamo Drafthouse would likely be involved in operating the complex.
	

	Alamo Drafthouse currently operates a 10-screen cinema in Downtown Los Angeles’ The Bloc complex. A 53-story residential complex has been approved to start construction as early as next year atop the Bloc’s parking garage, and although the cinemas will remain open, it’s unknown how construction will affect the mall’s businesses. 

	The Culver City studio had no comment. Decurion Corp, the parent company of owners Robertson Property Group, which is owned by the Forman family, likewise had no comment.

	Robertson Property Group has been conducting renovations in the theater off and on for the past several years, but there is currently no prospective opening date. In May, a liquor license renewal was approved at a city hearing, paving the way for reopening.

	The closure of the Dome and ArcLight Cinemas in Hollywood has been painful for both locals and industry creatives, leaving countless baffled that powerbrokers in town have not found a way to open the doors again.
	

	“Those theaters have sat dormant for six years,” one top movie theater exec groaned to Variety earlier this year at CinemaCon. “Imagine the mold.”

	The Blue Note Jazz Club recently opened an L.A. location in the Dome complex, with 100-seat and 200-seat theaters.

	Pacific Theatres, which later declared bankruptcy, closed the ArcLight chain and the Cinerama Dome permanently a year after COVID lockdowns suspended movie theater operations. Although several of the ArcLight locations were taken over by other operators, the Dome and ArcLight multiplex in Hollywood, which are owned outright by Pacific’s parent company Decurion, have been sitting empty ever since.

	The Cinerama Dome opened in 1963, originally as a showcase for the three-panel Cinerama process. Until its closure, it was one of the city’s highest-grossing multiplexes and a favorite stop for splashy premieres and events like a special preview screening of Quentin Tarantino’s “Once Upon a Time in Hollywood.”

	Los Angeles theaters have undergone big changes since the pandemic. Landmark Theatres closed its Pico Boulevard location in 2022, and the Bruin and Fox theaters in Westwood Village closed in 2024. The Fox was taken over by a consortium of filmmakers led by Jason Reitman, who is working on remodeling the venue and has opened it to screenings this summer in advance of the remodeling as the Director’s Village.

	— Matt Donnelly contributed to this report.

schema.headline: Sony Pictures Mulls Deal for Hollywood’s Cinerama Dome, With Alamo Drafthouse Possibly Operating

title: ‘The Odyssey’ Anoints Christopher Nolan as Hollywood’s Most Powerful Director — Here’s How Universal Thwarted Warner Bros. to Land Him
description: Greek mythology is riddled with humans who become immortal, many as punishment for tangling with the gods. But not Christopher Nolan, the toast of t
body:
Greek mythology is riddled with humans who become immortal, many as punishment for tangling with the gods. But not Christopher Nolan, the toast of the mortal world’s Mount Olympus — Hollywood.
	The opening-weekend success of his Imax epic “The Odyssey” cements Nolan as his own kind of myth: a bankable auteur who can command the attention of worldwide moviegoers simply by picking up his camera.Nolan has spent most of his career in the upper stratosphere of show business; he’s thought to be one of a few name directors who are events unto themselves (Quentin Tarantino, Ryan Coogler, Greta Gerwig, James Cameron). But after a nearly $265 million global first frame for “The Odyssey,” the best start of Nolan’s career, he’s moved even closer to the sun. Analysts predict the film now has the runway to gross $1 billion around the world. The title has also crashed down on this year’s Oscars race like a thunderbolt from Zeus, disrupting a low-hum narrative that Amazon MGM’s “Project Hail Mary” is the reigning, populist choice for best picture.“The Odyssey” has “invited people back to a theatrical experience which had been hobbled since the pandemic,” says Jason E. Squire, professor emeritus at USC School of Cinematic Arts. “This is an extraordinary creative and commercial victory for Nolan and Universal Pictures.”Indeed, the other victor here is Donna Langley, NBCUniversal’s entertainment and studios chairman. As the days tick by and box office records for “The Odyssey” keep rolling in, it’s abundantly clear how important it was that Langley wooed Nolan to Universal Pictures five years ago following his messy breakup with Warner Bros.In 2021, Langley fended off Sony Pictures, Paramount and the deep-pocketed Apple Studios to win the director’s unexpected blockbuster “Oppenheimer.” While Nolan never has signed any kind of exclusive studio deal — and almost surely never will — Langley’s team has proven an able partner for the once-in-a-generation talent. In essence, they have replaced Warner Bros., where Nolan made nine of his 13 movies, as his default home.“You may think it’s an easy approval process to move forward with any Nolan movie, but it’s an enormous financial commitment. A $250 million budget, another $100 million at least for marketing? There is no guarantee of profitability, and that’s the intriguing and seductive gamble for movie executives,” added Squire, who taught Coogler at USC and hosts “The Movie Business Podcast.”That gamble has paid off and observably came down to an issue of trust. Nolan was incensed in 2021 when Warner Bros., his studio partner of nearly 20 years, went rogue amid the COVID-19 pandemic and decided to release a full slate of films concurrently on its streaming service HBO Max and in cineplexes. This directly impacted Nolan’s time-traveling action film “Tenet,” led by John David Washington and Robert Pattinson. Nolan’s brand exclusively celebrates movies made for theaters. Jason Kilar, the corporate overlord of Warners at the time, exploded the company’s goodwill with talent and agents in every corner of the business by forcing the studio’s titles to stream day and date.Langley, known for an English sort of grace under pressure and her bedside manner with filmmakers, swooped in with a complex and unusual deal for “Oppenheimer.” It guaranteed a lengthy exclusive theatrical window for the opus about the atomic bomb, committed in writing to a marketing spend equal to the film’s $100 million budget and awarded Nolan a generous portion of the film’s first-dollar box office grosses. Nolan earned a reported $100 million in fees and back end for the best picture Oscar winner. Langley and Nolan’s bond had been forged years before, as the two both have same-aged kids. While his “Odyssey” deal is thought to be a more standard agreement than the “Oppenheimer” pact, insiders say Nolan will again receive first-dollar gross. That means he stands to reap millions for his work on top of his salary early in “The Odyssey”’s theatrical run, instead of having to wait until the film is profitable. Reps for Universal Pictures and Nolan declined to comment.Not that WB, Universal’s neighbor in Burbank, didn’t put up a fight. In 2022, following Discovery’s purchase of Warner Bros., the film studio’s incoming leaders, Mike De Luca and Pamela Abdy, announced plainly in a Variety cover story that they were “hoping” to get Nolan back. Subsequently, we reported that De Luca and Abdy cut a seven-figure check to the director in 2022 — a payment in arrears for fees he waived in hopes of getting “Tenet” to be the first film shown in theaters reopening after coronavirus closures.That “little kiss,” as one source put it, didn’t work. Especially given the windfall that came to Nolan after Warner Bros. staked its own blockbuster “Barbie” on the same July 2023 release date that Universal had given “Oppenheimer.” While the competition ignited the box office in a world-class example of counterprogramming, the move was seen as baiting and antagonizing the director. And so Universal won the day again with “The Odyssey.” After the success of “Oppenheimer,” Nolan and partner Emma Thomas did not take “The Odyssey” out to other bidders. Langley traveled to Nolan and Thomas’ Hollywood Hills home and read the script with the pair, insiders say. Afterward, the group rolled directly into planning for the Matt Damon epic.Nolan can run the table with any studio partner he wants, but “The Odyssey” feels like his own homecoming to Langley’s lot. Shareholders, the creative community and global audiences are all pleased. Why test the movie gods?

schema.headline: ‘The Odyssey’ Anoints Christopher Nolan as Hollywood’s Most Powerful Director — Here’s How Universal Thwarted Warner Bros. to Land Him

title: Spain’s World Cup Win Breaks Ratings Records, Scores as Most-Watched Soccer Telecast in U.S. TV History
description: Spain’s victory over Argentina at Sunday’s FIFA World Cup 2026 final broke the record books in the United States — averaging 38,937,000 viewers watc
body:
Spain’s victory over Argentina at Sunday’s FIFA World Cup 2026 final broke the record books in the United States — averaging 38,937,000 viewers watch on Fox, making it the most-watched soccer telecast in U.S. TV history and most-watched FIFA World Cup final ever in the U.S., according to Nielsen.

	Meanwhile, on the Spanish language side, Telemundo and Peacock brought in 23.9 million viewers to the World Cup Final, also making it the most-watched soccer match ever in Spanish-language TV history.
	

	Combined, that makes for a record-breaking 62.8 million viewers watching the match on Sunday, July 19.


	On Fox, the numbers spiked at 5:45 p.m., when 51,685,000 viewers were watching as Spain secured the only goal of the game — cementing its 1-0 victory in extra time over Argentina.

	The final was up 132% vs. FIFA World Cup 2022 final in Qatar (France vs. Argentina, Sunday morning Dec. 18, 2022, averaging 16,784,000 viewers) and up 240% vs. the FIFA World Cup 2018 final in Russia, (France vs. Croatia, Sunday morning July 15, 2018, with 11,441,000 viewers)

	The thriller of a World Cup final was completely dominated by Spain, which controlled the ball from virtually the start of the match. But despite multiple shots by Spain, Argentina deftly defended its goal — forcing the match to go into extra time. Finally, in minute 106, substitute Ferran Torres scored a goal for Spain, securing them the World Cup victory and denying Argentina a back-to-back win.

	The 38,937,000 viewers audience for the Spain win beat what had previously been the most-watched soccer telecast ever on U.S. English language TV, set by the U.S. Men’s National Team’s loss to Belgium in the round of 16, which garnered 33,089,000 viewers.
	

	Besides the Spain win, the World Cup finals included its first-ever halftime show on the field, staged by FIFA and Global Citizen, with Coldplay’s Chris Martin curating the show (in his role as international curator of the Global Citizen Festival) and Done+Dusted producing. Among those who performed: Madonna, Justin Bieber, BTS, Shakira, Burna Boy, PS22 Chorus with Coldplay, the New York Philharmonic and the Simón Bolívar Symphony Orchestra of Venezuela conducted by Gustavo Dudamel, and the Muppets.

	The World Cup Final was a smash all over the world: As Variety previously reported, inside the winning country of Spain, the final averaged 15.7 million viewers and an extraordinary 87.1% audience share across RTVE’s La 1, regional broadcaster 3Cat, La 2 and DAZN, reaching a cumulative 20.1 million people.

	Last week, the two World Cup semifinal matches also broke records, including last Wednesday’s nailbiter between Argentina and England that averaged 15.063 million viewers on Fox, making it the most-watched FIFA Men’s World Cup semifinals match in English-language history.

	All told, the 2026 FIFA World Cup averaged 7.74 million viewers on Fox, FS1 and Tubi across 104 matches, up 116% vs. 2022 (3.59 million) and up 179% from 2018 (2.77 million). That made it the most-watched in U.S. TV history. Meanwhile, Telemundo and Peacock secured the most-watched World Cup in Spanish-language U.S. TV history, with 6.3 million viewers — up 143% from 2022 (2.6 million).

	On Saturday, the third place game between France and England brought in 13,130,000 viewers on Fox, making it the most-watched FIFA Men’s World Cup third-place telecast in U.S. history (beating 2010’s Uruguay vs. Germany match, which had 4,755,000 viewers). Telemundo/Peacock’s coverage of the France vs. England third place match averaged 7.7 million viewers, making it the most-watched Third Place game in Spanish-language media history (up 225% vs. 2022’s Croatia vs. Morocco, which had 2.4 million viewers).

	The 39-day World Cup delivered an audience and sponsorship windfall for Fox and Telemundo/Peacock. With the strong performance on Fox, rivals are now looking at potentially making a bid for the next World Cup (which Fox paid $485 million for in 2026). Among some reportedly interested suitors: Netflix, ESPN, YouTube and Apple.

schema.headline: Spain’s World Cup Win Breaks Ratings Records, Scores as Most-Watched Soccer Telecast in U.S. TV History

title: Dixon Dern, Influential Entertainment Lawyer, Dies at 97
description: Dixon Q. Dern, a longtime Hollywood attorney who helped shape modern entertainment law through many innovative business deals, died July 19. He was
body:
Dixon Q. Dern, a longtime Hollywood attorney who helped shape modern entertainment law through many innovative business deals, died July 19. He was 97. 

	Born in Colorado Springs, Colorado, Dern earned undergraduate and law degrees from Stanford University. He came to Los Angeles as television was becoming a major force in the 1950s, representing major clients like Lucille Ball and Desi Arnaz.


	One of his early clients was “Mr. Magoo” producer UPA Productions, and he went on to represent clients including Bob Hope, Jackie Gleason, The Jackson Five and the Television Academy.
	

	A founding partner of Dern, Mason, Swerdlow & Floum, he helped build the firm’s major entertainment law practice and played a role in the founding of Creative Artists Agency. In his over-seven-decade career, Dern became one of Hollywood’s top attorneys and pioneered groundbreaking business structures. 


	As head of the legal department, he helped structure the business affairs of Desilu Productions during the period when television syndication became an important economic model. 

	Dern served as an arbitrator and mediator in entertainment and labor disputes starting in 1967. He served as VP and a Member of the Board of Governors of the State Bar of California; President of the Beverly Hills Bar Association; Trustee of the Los Angeles County Bar Association; and President of the Los Angeles Copyright Society. In 2009, the Television Academy honored him with its Syd Cassyd Founders Award, recognizing Academy members who have made significant impact’s on the Academy.

	He is survived by his children, Chelsea Dern, Warren Dern, Peter Dern, Rosalin Bellante, and Greg Dern, five grandchildren and many great-grandchildren.

schema.headline: Dixon Dern, Influential Entertainment Lawyer, Dies at 97

title: Guess The 'Crazy Rich Asian' Star Showing Off Her Abs
description:
Guess Who
                          'Crazy Rich Asians' Star With Sexy Abs
body:
Guess Who
                          'Crazy Rich Asians' Star With Sexy Abs
                      
      
    
            Can you guess which hot actress flaunted her toned tummy on socials in this eye-popping shot?!?
      
    
            Her breakthrough role was on ABC's sitcom "Fresh Off the Boat," but you probably know her best as Rachel Chu from the "Crazy Rich Asians" movie.
      
    
            Can you guess who she is???

title: Robert Downey Jr.'s 11-Year-Old Daughter's TV Show Payday Revealed
description:
Robert Downey Jr.
                          Daughter's TV Payday Revealed
body:
Robert Downey Jr.
                          Daughter's TV Payday Revealed
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              July 21, 2026
                            5:30 PM PDT
                                                                            
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Robert Downey Jr.'s daughter Avri Downey is getting into the family business ... she's set to film a new television show ... and we found out how much she's being paid.
      
    
            TMZ obtained the minor's contract Avri signed for her role in an upcoming show, "Oswald," and it calls for her to be paid $1,246 for one day of shooting.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                                  
      
    
            The deal was signed last year, and her character was listed as "Family Photo Kid" ... so it sounds like a smaller role.
      
    
            Avri's daily rate is a far cry from her dad’s reported $100 Million salary in "Avengers: Doomsday" ... but, hey, we all gotta start somewhere.
      

            The contract also has a provision requiring all cast members to remain professional and not cause any scandal during production.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            The project Avri is starring in isn't completely clear, but all signs point to the Disney show "Oswald the Lucky Rabbit," which is being directed by Jon Favreau.
      
    
            Avri's involvement would make sense ... Jon is a close friend of Robert's ... directing a number of his 'Iron Man' films.
      
    
            Seems Jon is keepin' it in the family!

title: Jason Alexander Apologizes to Courtney Stodden Over Controversial Comedy Sketch
description:
Jason Alexander
                          'I'm Deeply Sorry' to Courtney Stodden
body:
Jason Alexander
                          'I'm Deeply Sorry' to Courtney Stodden
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              July 21, 2026
                            5:10 PM PDT
                                                                      |
                                                                Updated 
                                  July 21, 2026
                                5:43 PM PDT
                                    
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            Jason Alexander is apologizing to Courtney Stodden after she publicly called him out over a controversial 2012 comedy sketch filmed when she was still a minor.
      
    
            The "Seinfeld" star tells TMZ ... "Looking back at the comedy sketch in which Ms. Stodden and I participated in 2012, I completely agree that it was inappropriate and I truly regret it."
      
    
            He continues ... "But more importantly, I am deeply sorry for any harm or distress it has caused Ms. Stodden. I offer her my sincerest apologies."
      

        Waiting for your permission to load the Instagram Media.
      
    
            Alexander's apology comes after Stodden blasted him on Instagram, alleging during the 2012 Funny or Die sketch he repeatedly rubbed a cellphone across her chest while joking there was "no signal" in her head but "plenty of signal" in her chest. She also claimed he joked about taking her "behind the couch" and "having his way" with her once she turned 18.
      
    
            Stodden, now 31, said she was just 17 at the time and described herself as "the only child in the room." She noted Alexander was also credited as a writer on the sketch and questioned how so many adults believed the material was acceptable.
      
    
            She pushed back on criticism she willingly participated, arguing she was legally unable to make those decisions for herself because the contracts were signed by adults and the money went to her then-husband, Doug Hutchison, whom she married at 16.
      
    
            In recent years, Stodden has become an advocate against child marriage and has repeatedly spoken out about adults she says exploited her while she was underage.

title: Celeste Rivas' Family Lawyer Details 'Gruesome' Dead Body Photos, Says She Was 'Mush'
description:
Celeste Rivas' Family Lawyer
                          Details Dead Body as 'Mush' ...
body:
Celeste Rivas' Family Lawyer
                          Details Dead Body as 'Mush' ...
                          After Pics Shown At D4vd Hearing
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              July 21, 2026
                            6:26 PM PDT
                                                                            
      
    
        
                    
                  
      
    
            Celeste Rivas' body had liquefied and looked like "mush" in the graphic photos shown during D4vd's preliminary hearing Tuesday ... according to the 14-year-old's family's attorney.
      
    
            We caught up with Patrick Steinfeld outside court after the hearing, and he tells TMZ the alleged evidence presented against the singer is "damning" based on what he saw inside the courtroom.
      
    
      
        
              
    
    
            Steinfeld says the exhibits were so "extremely painful" for the family to sit through that Celeste's mother, Mercedes Martinez, sobbed throughout the hearing and "never recovered."
      

            He says one image shown in court was so graphic that Deputy District Attorney Beth Silverman asked an LAPD detective what she was looking at, and the detective responded it was Celeste's head. According to Steinfeld, the condition of the remains was so severe that you wouldn't be able to identify it as a person or a body.
      
    
            Steinfeld says D4vd deserves life in prison without the possibility of parole if he's convicted.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                                  
      
    
            As we previously reported, prosecutors allege D4vd left Celeste's dismembered body inside the Tesla registered to him after they claim he lured her to his Hollywood Hills rental home to kill her after they say she was going to expose their relationship.
      
    
            D4vd has pleaded not guilty to all charges brought against him -- first degree murder with special circumstances, including lying in wait, committing the crime for financial gain, and murdering a witness to an investigation, lewd and lascivious sexual acts with a person under 14 years old, and the mutilation of a human body.

title: Nolan Wells Case: New Video Potentially the Last Before He Went Missing
description:
Nolan Wells
                          New Video From Horn Island ...
                          Could B
body:
Nolan Wells
                          New Video From Horn Island ...
                          Could Be Last Image Before His Death
                      
                  
                                                                        Published 
                              
                July 21, 2026
                
                            1:00 AM PDT
                                                                      |
                                                                Updated 
                                  July 21, 2026
                                9:12 AM PDT
                                    
              
    
        
                    
                  
      
    
            This new video from the day Nolan Wells went missing might reveal the first clues about who he was with during the last hours of the Horn Island 4th of July party.
      
    
            TMZ obtained the video recorded that fateful day ... as the camera pans around to show multiple boats and tons of people partying, you can see a Black man standing on the back deck of a boat, apparently bending over to get a drink.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                                  
      
    
            Check the video ... the man is wearing trunks matching the color of the ones Nolan was wearing in the infamous July 4 pic with his friends on a boat.
      

            You can also see a couple of older adults also onboard the boat, which is anchored in neck-deep water ... and there are several people hanging out nearby. Metadata shows the video was recorded at 2:23 PM.
      
    
            While the video does not show the face of the guy on the back of the boat, the person who shot it felt strongly enough it could be Nolan that they went to cops.
      
    
            We're told they brought the video to the Jackson County Sheriff's Office last week, and left a copy with a detective working the case.
      
    
            A law enforcement source confirmed to TMZ ... investigators are aware of the clip. If it is Nolan, this video would be the last known image of the 18-year-old.
      
    
            About 2 hours later, the boat Nolan's friends were on left the island -- around 4:30 PM according to GPS -- and returned to the Mississippi mainland without him.
      
    
                  
          
                          
                
                  
      
    
            As you know, Nolan disappeared after those friends left, and his body was found July 6 on the Horn Island shoreline. No witnesses have, at least publicly, reported seeing him after 4:30 PM on July 4.
      
    
            Nolan's family is conducting its own investigation into the 18-year-old's mysterious death ... including ordering an independent autopsy.
      
    
        
                    
                  
      
    
            The boy's parents laid him to rest Monday at Center Pointe Church in Ocean Springs, Mississippi, in a service officiated by Rev. Al Sharpton ... where he and Tyler Perry each offered $50K for information leading to a conviction in Nolan's death.

schema.headline: Nolan Wells Case: New Video Potentially the Last Before He Went Missing
